revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''石人石馬'''（せきじんせきば）とは、[[古墳]]の墳丘に立て並べられた人物や馬をはじめとする石造彫刻の総称であ...'
revision.text:
'''石人石馬'''（せきじんせきば）とは、[[古墳]]の墳丘に立て並べられた人物や馬をはじめとする石造彫刻の総称である。 [[ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|石馬（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館]] == 概要 == [[5世紀]]～[[6世紀]]の[[福岡県]]・[[大分県]]・[[熊本県]]を中心にみられる。種類は人物（武装石人・裸体石人など）、動物（馬・鶏・猪など）、器財（靫・盾・刀・壷・蓋など）があり、赤や緑で彩色されているものもある。その種類が[[形象埴輪]]と種類が共通することから、埴輪を石にうつしたものと考えられている。 主として阿蘇溶結凝灰岩が使われるが、[[鳥取県]][[石馬谷古墳]]のものは[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使われていた。 石人石馬が種類・数ともに最も多いのは[[八女古墳群]]の[[岩戸山古墳]]で、人・動物・器財あわせて100点以上が掘り出されている == 石人石馬が出土した古墳 == * [[石馬谷古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） - 石馬（国の重要文化財） * [[岩戸山古墳]]（[[福岡県]][[八女市]]） - 石人・石馬・石鶏・石猪・石盾・石壺・石刀・石靫・石蓋（国の重要文化財） * [[乗場古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人 * [[鶴見山古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石人山古墳]]（福岡県[[八女郡]][[広川町]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石神山古墳]]（福岡県[[みやま市]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[臼塚古墳]]（[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]） - 石甲（国の重要文化財） * [[下山古墳 (大分県)|下山古墳]]（大分県臼杵市） - 石甲 * [[チブサン古墳]]（[[熊本県]][[山鹿市]]） - 石人 * [[三の宮古墳]]（熊本県[[荒尾市]]） - 石人 * [[江田船山古墳]]（熊本県[[玉名郡]][[和水町]]） - 石人 == ギャラリー == ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石壺.jpg|石壺（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石盾.jpg|石盾（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石盾 2.jpg|石盾（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 1.jpg|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 2.jpg|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:Sekijin-01.jpg|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
[[東京国立博物館]] ファイル:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|武装石人（鶴見山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 == 参考文献 == * [[大塚初重]]・[[戸沢充則]]（編集）『最新日本考古学用語辞典』新創社、1996年6月、175頁 * [[田村晃一]]・合田芳正（監修）『考古学探訪の基礎用語』[[山川出版社]]、2000年7月、73-74頁 == 関連項目 == * [[石像]] * [[猿石]] == 外部リンク == * [http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/museumcat/stone　石人石馬(石製表飾品)　岩戸山歴史資料館] * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/aboutyame/bunka/k_i_002.html　石製表飾品　八女市] * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/aboutyame/bunka/k_i_003.html　豊福の石人　八女市]

'''石人石馬'''（せきじんせきば）とは、[[古墳]]の墳丘に立て並べられた人物や馬をはじめとする石造彫刻の総称である。 [[ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|石馬（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館]] == 概要 == [[5世紀]]～[[6世紀]]の[[福岡県]]・[[大分県]]・[[熊本県]]を中心にみられる。種類は人物（武装石人・裸体石人など）、動物（馬・鶏・猪など）、器財（靫・盾・刀・壷・蓋など）があり、赤や緑で彩色されているものもある。その種類が[[形象埴輪]]と種類が共通することから、埴輪を石にうつしたものと考えられている。 主として阿蘇溶結凝灰岩が使われるが、[[鳥取県]][[石馬谷古墳]]のものは[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使われていた。 石人石馬が種類・数ともに最も多いのは[[八女古墳群]]の[[岩戸山古墳]]で、人・動物・器財あわせて100点以上が掘り出されている == 石人石馬が出土した古墳 == * [[石馬谷古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） - 石馬（国の重要文化財） * [[岩戸山古墳]]（[[福岡県]][[八女市]]） - 石人・石馬・石鶏・石猪・石盾・石壺・石刀・石靫・石蓋（国の重要文化財） * [[乗場古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人 * [[鶴見山古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石人山古墳]]（福岡県[[八女郡]][[広川町]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石神山古墳]]（福岡県[[みやま市]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[臼塚古墳]]（[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]） - 石甲（国の重要文化財） * [[下山古墳 (大分県)|下山古墳]]（大分県臼杵市） - 石甲 * [[チブサン古墳]]（[[熊本県]][[山鹿市]]） - 石人 * [[三の宮古墳]]（熊本県[[荒尾市]]） - 石人 * [[江田船山古墳]]（熊本県[[玉名郡]][[和水町]]） - 石人 == ギャラリー == ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石壺.jpg|石壺（岩戸山古墳）

revision.text:
'''石人石馬'''（せきじんせきば）とは、[[古墳]]の墳丘に立て並べられた人物や馬をはじめとする石造彫刻の総称である。 [[ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|石馬（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館]] == 概要 == [[5世紀]]～[[6世紀]]の[[福岡県]]・[[大分県]]・[[熊本県]]を中心にみられる。種類は人物（武装石人・裸体石人など）、動物（馬・鶏・猪など）、器財（靫・盾・刀・壷・蓋など）があり、赤や緑で彩色されているものもある。その種類が[[形象埴輪]]と種類が共通することから、埴輪を石にうつしたものと考えられている。 主として阿蘇溶結凝灰岩が使われるが、[[鳥取県]][[石馬谷古墳]]のものは[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使われていた。 石人石馬が種類・数ともに最も多いのは[[八女古墳群]]の[[岩戸山古墳]]で、人・動物・器財あわせて100点以上が掘り出されている == 石人石馬が出土した古墳 == * [[石馬谷古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） - 石馬（国の重要文化財） * [[岩戸山古墳]]（[[福岡県]][[八女市]]） - 石人・石馬・石鶏・石猪・石盾・石壺・石刀・石靫・石蓋（国の重要文化財） * [[乗場古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人 * [[鶴見山古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石人山古墳]]（福岡県[[八女郡]][[広川町]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石神山古墳]]（福岡県[[みやま市]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[臼塚古墳]]（[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]） - 石甲（国の重要文化財） * [[下山古墳 (臼杵市)|下山古墳]]（大分県臼杵市） - 石甲 * [[チブサン古墳]]（[[熊本県]][[山鹿市]]） - 石人 * [[三の宮古墳]]（熊本県[[荒尾市]]） - 石人 * [[江田船山古墳]]（熊本県[[玉名郡]][[和水町]]） - 石人 == ギャラリー == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|武装石人（石人山古墳） ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石壺.jpg|石壺（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石盾.jpg|石盾（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石盾 2.jpg|石盾（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 1.jpg|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 2.jpg|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:Sekijin-01.jpg|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
[[東京国立博物館]] ファイル:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|武装石人（鶴見山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 == 参考文献 == * [[大塚初重]]・[[戸沢充則]]（編集）『最新日本考古学用語辞典』新創社、1996年6月、175頁 * [[田村晃一]]・合田芳正（監修）『考古学探訪の基礎用語』[[山川出版社]]、2000年7月、73-74頁 == 関連項目 == * [[石像]] * [[猿石]] * [[飛鳥の石造物]] == 外部リンク == * [http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/museumcat/stone　石人石馬(石製表飾品)　岩戸山歴史資料館] * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/aboutyame/bunka/k_i_002.html　石製表飾品　八女市] * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/aboutyame/bunka/k_i_003.html　豊福の石人　八女市] {{DEFAULTSORT:せきしんせきは}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:考古学]] [[Category:古墳]]

'''石人石馬'''（せきじんせきば）とは、[[古墳]]の墳丘に立て並べられた人物や馬をはじめとする石造彫刻の総称である。 [[ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|石馬（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館]] == 概要 == [[5世紀]]～[[6世紀]]の[[福岡県]]・[[大分県]]・[[熊本県]]を中心にみられる。種類は人物（武装石人・裸体石人など）、動物（馬・鶏・猪など）、器財（靫・盾・刀・壷・蓋など）があり、赤や緑で彩色されているものもある。その種類が[[形象埴輪]]と種類が共通することから、埴輪を石にうつしたものと考えられている。 主として阿蘇溶結凝灰岩が使われるが、[[鳥取県]][[石馬谷古墳]]のものは[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使われていた。 石人石馬が種類・数ともに最も多いのは[[八女古墳群]]の[[岩戸山古墳]]で、人・動物・器財あわせて100点以上が掘り出されている == 石人石馬が出土した古墳 == * [[石馬谷古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） - 石馬（国の重要文化財） * [[岩戸山古墳]]（[[福岡県]][[八女市]]） - 石人・石馬・石鶏・石猪・石盾・石壺・石刀・石靫・石蓋（国の重要文化財） * [[乗場古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人 * [[鶴見山古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石人山古墳]]（福岡県[[八女郡]][[広川町]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石神山古墳]]（福岡県[[みやま市]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[臼塚古墳]]（[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]） - 石甲（国の重要文化財） * [[下山古墳 (臼杵市)|下山古墳]]（大分県臼杵市） - 石甲 * [[チブサン古墳]]（[[熊本県]][[山鹿市]]） - 石人 * [[三の宮古墳]]（熊本県[[荒尾市]]） - 石人 * [[江田船山古墳]]（熊本県[[玉名郡]][[和水町]]） - 石人 == ギャラリー == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|武装石人（石人山古墳） ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石壺.jpg|石壺（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石盾.jpg|石盾（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石盾 2.jpg|石盾（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 1.jpg|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 2.jpg|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:Sekijin-01.jpg|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
[[東京国立博物館]] ファイル:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|武装石人（鶴見山古墳）

'''石人石馬'''（せきじんせきば）とは、[[古墳]]の墳丘に立て並べられた人物や馬をはじめとする石造彫刻の総称である。 [[ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|石馬（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館]] == 概要 == [[5世紀]]～[[6世紀]]の[[福岡県]]・[[大分県]]・[[熊本県]]を中心にみられる。種類は人物（武装石人・裸体石人など）、動物（馬・鶏・猪など）、器財（靫・盾・刀・壷・蓋など）があり、赤や緑で彩色されているものもある。その種類が[[形象埴輪]]と種類が共通することから、埴輪を石にうつしたものと考えられている。 主として阿蘇溶結凝灰岩が使われるが、[[鳥取県]][[石馬谷古墳]]のものは[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使われていた。 石人石馬が種類・数ともに最も多いのは[[八女古墳群]]の[[岩戸山古墳]]で、人・動物・器財あわせて100点以上が掘り出されている == 石人石馬が出土した古墳 == * [[石馬谷古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） - 石馬（国の重要文化財） * [[岩戸山古墳]]（[[福岡県]][[八女市]]） - 石人・石馬・石鶏・石猪・石盾・石壺・石刀・石靫・石蓋（国の重要文化財） * [[乗場古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人 * [[鶴見山古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石人山古墳]]（福岡県[[八女郡]][[広川町 (福岡県)|広川町]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石神山古墳]]（福岡県[[みやま市]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[臼塚古墳]]（[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]） - 石甲（国の重要文化財） * [[下山古墳 (臼杵市)|下山古墳]]（大分県臼杵市） - 石甲 * [[チブサン古墳]]（[[熊本県]][[山鹿市]]） - 石人 * [[三の宮古墳]]（熊本県[[荒尾市]]） - 石人 * [[江田船山古墳]]（熊本県[[玉名郡]][[和水町]]） - 石人 == ギャラリー == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|武装石人（石人山古墳） ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石壺.jpg|石壺（岩戸山古墳）

revision.text:
[[ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|石馬（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館]] '''石人石馬'''（せきじんせきば）は、[[九州]]北部の[[古墳]]の墳丘に立て並べられた、人物や馬をはじめとする石造彫刻の総称。 == 概要 == [[5世紀]]〜[[6世紀]]の[[福岡県]]・[[大分県]]・[[熊本県]]の古墳を中心にみられる。種類は人物（武装石人・裸体石人など）、動物（馬・鶏・猪など）、器財（靫・盾・刀・壷・蓋など）があり、赤や緑で彩色されているものもある。その種類が[[形象埴輪]]と種類が共通することから、埴輪を石にうつしたものと考えられている。 主として阿蘇溶結凝灰岩が使われるが、[[鳥取県]][[石馬谷古墳]]のものは[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使われていた。 石人石馬が種類・数ともに最も多いのは[[八女古墳群]]の[[岩戸山古墳]]で、人・動物・器財あわせて100点以上が掘り出されている == 石人石馬が出土した古墳 == * [[石馬谷古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） - 石馬（国の重要文化財） * [[岩戸山古墳]]（[[福岡県]][[八女市]]） - 石人・石馬・石鶏・石猪・石盾・石壺・石刀・石靫・石蓋（国の重要文化財） * [[乗場古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人 * [[鶴見山古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石人山古墳]]（福岡県[[八女郡]][[広川町 (福岡県)|広川町]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[石神山古墳]]（福岡県[[みやま市]]） - 石人（国の重要文化財） * [[臼塚古墳]]（[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]） - 石甲（国の重要文化財） * [[下山古墳 (臼杵市)|下山古墳]]（大分県臼杵市） - 石甲 * [[チブサン古墳]]（[[熊本県]][[山鹿市]]） - 石人 * [[三の宮古墳]]（熊本県[[荒尾市]]） - 石人 * [[江田船山古墳]]（熊本県[[玉名郡]][[和水町]]） - 石人 == ギャラリー == ファイル:石人山古墳石人.JPG|武装石人（石人山古墳） ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石壺.jpg|石壺（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石盾.jpg|石盾（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 石盾 2.jpg|石盾（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 1.jpg|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 2.jpg|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 ファイル:岩戸山古墳出土 石人-2.JPG|武装石人（岩戸山古墳）
[[東京国立博物館]] ファイル:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|武装石人（鶴見山古墳）
岩戸山歴史資料館 == 参考文献 == * [[大塚初重]]・[[戸沢充則]]（編集）『最新日本考古学用語辞典』新創社、1996年6月、175頁 * [[田村晃一]]・合田芳正（監修）『考古学探訪の基礎用語』[[山川出版社]]、2000年7月、73-74頁 == 関連項目 == * [[石像]] * [[猿石]] * [[飛鳥の石造物]] * [[埴輪]] == 外部リンク == * [http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/museumcat/stone　石人石馬(石製表飾品)　岩戸山歴史資料館] * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/aboutyame/bunka/k_i_002.html　石製表飾品　八女市] * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/aboutyame/bunka/k_i_003.html　豊福の石人　八女市] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せきしんせきは}} [[Category:石像]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:日本の考古資料]] [[Category:古墳]] [[Category:美術におけるウマ]]

福岡県指定有形文化財]] '''石人石馬'''（せきじんせきば）は、[[九州]]北部の[[古墳]]の墳丘に立て並べられた、人物や馬をはじめとする石造彫刻の総称。 == 概要 == [[5世紀]]〜[[6世紀]]の[[福岡県]]・[[大分県]]・[[熊本県]]の古墳を中心にみられる。種類は人物（武装石人・裸体石人など）、動物（馬・鶏・猪など）、器財（靫・盾・刀・壷・蓋など）があり、赤や緑で彩色されているものもある。その種類が[[形象埴輪]]と種類が共通することから、埴輪を石にうつしたものと考えられている。 主として阿蘇溶結凝灰岩が使われるが、[[鳥取県]][[石馬谷古墳]]のものは[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使われていた。 石人石馬が種類・数ともに最も多いのは[[八女古墳群]]の[[岩戸山古墳]]で、人・動物・器財あわせて100点以上が掘り出されている == 石人石馬が出土した古墳 == * [[石馬谷古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） - 石馬（[[重要文化財]]） * [[岩戸山古墳]]（[[福岡県]][[八女市]]） - 石人・石馬・石鶏・石猪・石盾・石壺・石刀・石靫・石蓋（重要文化財） * [[乗場古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人 * [[鶴見山古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人（重要文化財） * [[石人山古墳]]（福岡県[[八女郡]][[広川町 (福岡県)|広川町]]） - 石人（重要文化財） * [[石神山古墳]]（福岡県[[みやま市]]） - 石人（重要文化財） * [[臼塚古墳]]（[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]） - 石甲（重要文化財） * [[下山古墳 (臼杵市)|下山古墳]]（大分県臼杵市） - 石甲 * [[チブサン古墳]]（[[熊本県]][[山鹿市]]） - 石人 * [[三の宮古墳]]（熊本県[[荒尾市]]） - 石人 * [[江田船山古墳]]（熊本県[[玉名郡]][[和水町]]） - 石人 ※「重要文化財」は文化財保護法に基づいて指定された物件（「国の重要文化財」） == ギャラリー == ファイル:石人山古墳石人.JPG|武装石人（石人山古墳）

revision.text:
[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures **Note**: Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)
Owned by Hirokawa Town
Important Cultural Property File:岩戸山古墳 石壺.jpg|Stone Pot (Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Yame City (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Important Cultural Property File:岩戸山古墳 石盾.jpg|Stone Shield (Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Yame City (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Important Cultural Property File:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 1.jpg|Armed Stone Figure (Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|Armed Stone Figure (Tsurumiyama Kofun)
Owned by Yame City (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Important Cultural Property == References == * {{ill|Hatsushige Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Mitsunori Tozawa (eds.), *Latest Dictionary of Japanese Archaeological Terms*, Shinso Publishing, June 1996, pp. 175 * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Gōda (supervisors), *Basic Terms for Archaeological Exploration*, Yamakawa Publishing, July 2000, pp. 73–74 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Stone statues|ja|石像}} * {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} * {{ill|Asuka Stone Works|ja|飛鳥の石造物}} * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} == External Links == {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Stone statues]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Archaeological artifacts in Japan]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Horses in art]] [[ja:石人石馬]]

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures **Note**: Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

revision.text:
[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)
Owned by Hirokawa Town
Important Cultural Property File:岩戸山古墳 石壺.jpg|Stone Pot (Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Yame City (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Important Cultural Property File:岩戸山古墳 石盾.jpg|Stone Shield (Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Yame City (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Important Cultural Property File:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 1.jpg|Armed Stone Figure (Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|Armed Stone Figure (Tsurumiyama Kofun)
Owned by Yame City (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Important Cultural Property == References == * {{ill|Hatsushige Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Mitsunori Tozawa (eds.), *Latest Dictionary of Japanese Archaeological Terms*, Shinso Publishing, June 1996, pp. 175 * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Gōda (supervisors), *Basic Terms for Archaeological Exploration*, Yamakawa Publishing, July 2000, pp. 73–74 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Stone sculpture|ja|石像}} * {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} * {{ill|Asuka Stone Works|ja|飛鳥の石造物}} * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} == External Links == {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone statues]] [[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)
Owned by Hirokawa Town

The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. [[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)
