[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. [[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)
Owned by Hirokawa Town
Important Cultural Property File:岩戸山古墳 石壺.jpg|Stone Pot (Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Yame City (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Important Cultural Property File:岩戸山古墳 石盾.jpg|Stone Shield (Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Yame City (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)

Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)

Owned by Yame City (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)
Owned by Hirokawa Town

Owned by Yame City (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Important Cultural Property File:岩戸山古墳 武装石人 1.jpg|Armed Stone Figure (Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|Armed Stone Figure (Tsurumiyama Kofun)
Owned by Yame City (preserved at Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

Important Cultural Property == References == * {{ill|Hatsushige Ōtsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Mitsunori Tozawa (eds.), *Latest Dictionary of Japanese Archaeological Terms*, Shinso Publishing, June 1996, 11. 175 * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Gōda (supervisors), *Basic Terms for Archaeological Exploration*, Yamakawa Publishing, July 2000, 11. 73–74 == See Also == * {{ill|Stone sculpture|ja|石像}} * {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} * {{ill|Asuka Stone Works|ja|飛鳥の石造物}} * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} == External Links == {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Stone statues]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Archaeological artifacts in Japan]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Horses in art]] [[Category:Stone human and horse figures]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[Category:Stone human and horse figures]][[Category:Sekijin-sekiba]][[Category:Stone human and horse figures]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[Category:Stone human and horse figures]][[Category:Sekijin-sekiba]][[Category:Stone human and horse figures]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[Category:Stone human and horse figures]][[Category:Stone human and horse figures]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:tone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]] [[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]] [[ja:石人石馬]] [[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[en:Stone human and horse figures]][[en:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{wikidata link|Q17994947}} ==English article== {{Short description|Type of Japanese archaeological artefact}} {{Italic title}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2025}} [[File:岩戸山古墳出土 石人 (J-831).JPG|thumb|right|6th-century {{tlit|ja|sekijin}} ([[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ICP]]) from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] ([[Tokyo National Museum]]){{cite web |url=https://emuseum.nich.go.jp/detail?content_base_id=100566 |script-title=ja:石人 |trans-title=Stone figure |language=ja, en |publisher=[[National Institutes for Cultural Heritage]] |access-date=6 March 2025}}]] {{nihongo||石人石馬|'''Sekijin sekiba'''|lead=yes}} is the inclusive term for [[sculpture]]s in stone from the [[Kofun|ancient burial mounds]] of [[Kofun period|Kofun-period]] Japan. It includes not only the stone figures of humans and horses denoted by the [[kanji]], but also depictions of other animals and objects. For this reason, these sculptures are sometimes referred to alternatively as {{nihongo||石製表飾|sekisei hyōshoku}}.{{cite book |chapter-url=https://miyazaki-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/2228 |script-title=ja:石人・石馬: 里帰り展 |trans-title=''Sekijin Sekiba'': Homecoming Exhibition |script-chapter=ja:筑紫の石製表飾(石人石馬)と古墳文化 |trans-chapter=Stone Embellishments (''Sekijin Sekiba'') of Tsukushi and Kofun Culture |author-last=Yanagisawa |author-first=Kazuo |publisher=Iwatoyama History Museum |year=1994 |pages=63–71}} ==Overview== {{tlit|ja|Sekijin sekiba}} are found mainly in northern and central [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] ([[Chikugo Province|Chikugo]], [[Higo Province|Higo]], and [[Bungo Province|Bungo]]{{cite web |url=https://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/soshiki/17/1/2/iwatoyamakouhouyame/1504594652623.html |script-title=ja:岩戸山古墳の石製表飾“石人・石馬” |trans-title=''Sekijin sekiba'' from Iwatoyama Kofun |language=ja |publisher=[[Yame|Yame City]] |access-date=7 March 2025}}) and date from the mid- to late-[[Kofun period]] ([[Century|C]]5–C6).{{rp|63}}{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031532/index.html |script-title=ja:西原古墳出土石製表飾遺物 |trans-title=Stone Embellishments Excavated from Nishihara Kofun |language=ja |publisher=[[Saga Prefecture]] |access-date=6 March 2025 |date=25 April 2023 }} Ranging in height from around {{convert|2|m}} to {{convert|30|cm}},{{rp|63}} they are understood to have been erected in front of the [[Sekkan|stone coffin]] or on top of {{tlit|ja|[[kofun]]}} together with or in place of clay {{tlit|ja|[[haniwa]]}}. These may have influenced their development—as did, in all likelihood, the statues lining the [[spirit path]]s of tombs in China.{{cite book |title=Early Japanese Art: The Great Tombs and Treasures |author-last=Kidder |first=J. Edward Jr. |author-link=J. Edward Kidder Jr. |publisher=[[Thames & Hudson]] |year=1964 |pages=82,92–94,194–195}} More than a hundred are known from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Yame]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], and sixteen from [[Nozu Kofun Cluster#Hime-no-jō Kofun|Hime-no-jō Kofun]] in [[Kumamoto Prefecture]];{{cite web |url=https://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/iwatoyama/iwaiwoshiru/11763.html |script-title=ja:4.岩戸山古墳が世界最多！「石人・石馬（石製表飾）」|trans-title=4. Iwatoyama Kofun, the most in the world! "''Sekijin sekiba (sekisei hyōshoku)''" |language=ja |publisher=[[Yame|Yame City]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} elsewhere, they are found singly or in low numbers.{{cite web |url=https://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/soshiki/4/6/3/1454651828276.html |script-title=ja:石製表飾品 |trans-title=Stone Embellishments |language=ja |publisher=[[Yame|Yame City]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} Most are carved from [[ignimbrite]] from [[Mount Aso]], though an example from [[Yonago, Tottori|Yonago]] in western Honshū is of [[andesite]] from [[Mount Daisen]].{{cite web |url=https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm |script-title=ja:国指定重要文化財 石馬 |trans-title=Nationally-designated Important Cultural Property: ''Sekiba'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Yonago, Tottori|Yonago City]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} Some retain traces of red and green pigments. Over forty examples had been catalogued by the early 1900s and in 1929 archaeologist {{ill|Morimoto Rokuji|ja|森本六爾}} published his [[monograph]] ''Sekijin Sekiba''.{{cite web |url=https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1130000796100317568 |script-title=ja:石人石馬 |trans-title=''Sekijin Sekiba'' |language=ja |website=[[CiNii]] |access-date=7 March 2025}} Suggestions as to their function include [[propitiation]] of the [[Spirit (animating force)|spirits]] of the dead, comforting the same through the accompaniment of familiar figures and things, protection of the tomb and the symbolic punishment of [[Grave robbery|robbers]], and, in the case of Iwatoyama Kofun with its "sixty stone men and sixty stone shields" {{lang|ja|「石人石盾各々六〇枚」}} per [[Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] {{tlit|ja|[[Fudoki]]}},{{rp|65}} the expression of [[Power (social and political)|power]]. Perhaps for this reason, after the [[Iwai Rebellion]] they appear to have declined somewhat in popularity, perhaps supplanted in part by the paintings of the so-called [[decorated kofun|decorated {{tlit|ja|kofun}}]]. ==Types== {{tlit|ja|Sekijin sekiba}} include sculptures of men, many of them armed, and, less commonly, women, in one instance with a child on her back;{{rp|66}} animals, including horses, boar, chickens, and waterfowl; {{tlit|ja|[[tankō]]}} and other [[Japanese armour|armour]], shields, swords, quivers, headpieces, vases, lids, a house, a chair, and a boat. ==Distribution== {{tlit|ja|Sekijin sekiba}} are known from over twenty-five sites, all but one of them in Kyūshū, the exception being {{ill|Ishiumadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in [[Yonago]], [[Tottori Prefecture]].{{rp|70}} Within Kyūshū, all but four are in the [[Prefectures of Japan|prefectures]] of [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] and [[Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto]], two being in [[Ōita Prefecture|Ōita]] ([[Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|Shimoyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} in [[Usuki, Ōita|Usuki]]), with [[Saga Prefecture|Saga]] ({{ill|Nishihara Kofun|qid=Q133105161}} in [[Saga (city)|Saga]]) and [[Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki]] ([[Nobeoka]]) having one apiece.{{rp|70}} Of the ten sites in Fukuoka Prefecture, there is a concentration in [[Yame]] ([[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Noriba Kofun|Noriba Kofun]], {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}}, Dōnanzan No. 3 Kofun, Dōnanzan No. 22 Kofun, Toyofuku), the others being in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa]] ([[Yame Kofun Cluster#Sekijinsan Kofun|Sekijinsan Kofun]]), [[Kurume]] ([[Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|Ontsuka Kofun]]), [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]] ([[Sekijinsan Kofun]]), and [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]].{{rp|70}} The eleven sites in Kumamoto are in [[Arao, Kumamoto|Arao]] ({{nihongo|[[Sannomiya Kofun]]|三の宮古墳}}), [[Hikawa, Kumamoto|Hikawa]] ([[Nozu Kofun Cluster#Amazutsu Kofun|Amazutsu Kofun]] and [[Nozu Kofun Cluster#Hime-no-jō Kofun|Hime-no-jō Kofun]]), [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]] ({{nihongo|[[Futatsukasan Kofun]]|フタツカサン古墳}} and Onozaki), [[Kumamoto]] ({{nihongo|[[Ishinomuro Kofun]]|石之室古墳}}), {{nihongo|[[Tominoo Kofun]]|富ノ尾古墳}}, and Kitahara No. 1 Kofun), [[Nagomi, Kumamoto|Nagomi]] ([[Eta Funayama Kofun]]), and [[Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga]] ([[Chibusan-Obusan Kofun|Chibusan Kofun]] and [[Usuzuka Kofun (Yamaga)|Usuzuka Kofun]]).{{rp|70}} ==Important Cultural Properties== Several {{tlit|ja|sekijin sekiba}} have been [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * ''Sekijin'' (stone man) from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] ([[Tokyo National Museum]]){{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9962 |script-title=ja:石人 |trans-title=''Sekijin'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} * Armed ''sekijin'' from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Sekijinsan Kofun|Sekijinsan Kofun]] ([[Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa]]), Fukuoka Prefecture{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9963 |script-title=ja:武装石人 |trans-title=Armed ''Sekijin'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} * Armed ''sekijin'' from [[Sekijinsan Kofun]] ([[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]]), Fukuoka Prefecture{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9964 |script-title=ja:武装石人 |trans-title=Armed ''Sekijin'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} * Armed ''sekijin'' from {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}}, Fukuoka Prefecture ([[Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Community Center]]){{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011450 |script-title=ja:武装石人／福岡県鶴見山古墳出土 |trans-title=Armed ''Sekijin'' Excavated from Tsurumiyama Kofun, Fukuoka Prefecture |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} * 62 pieces from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] (Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Community Center){{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011206 |script-title=ja:石人／石靱／石盾　附　石人等残欠 |trans-title=''Sekijin'', Stone Quiver, and Stone Shields, together with fragments of ''Sekijin'', etc. |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}}{{cite web |url=https://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/iwatoyama/collections/tennjihin_syuuzouhin/1485415259288.html |script-title=ja:石人・石馬類 |trans-title=''Sekijin・Sekiba'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Yame|Yame City]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} * ''Sekiba'' (stone horse) from {{ill|Ishiumadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in [[Yonago]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] (Amenokamigaki Jinja){{rp|70}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9815 |script-title=ja:石馬 |trans-title=''Sekiba'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} ==Gallery== 鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|''Sekijin'' (stone man) from {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] 岩戸山古墳 武装石人 2.jpg|''Sekijin'' from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], Fukuoka Prefecture 岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|''Sekiba'' (stone horse) from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], Fukuoka Prefecture 石馬谷古墳出土 石馬 (複製).JPG|''Sekiba'' (replica) from {{ill|Ishiumadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]] ([[Kamiyodo Hakuhō-no-Oka Exhibition Hall]]) 岩戸山古墳 石盾.jpg|Shield from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], Fukuoka Prefecture ==See also== 福岡県指定有形文化財]] '''石人石馬'''（せきじんせきば）は、[[九州]]北部の[[古墳]]の墳丘に立て並べられた、人物や馬をはじめとする石造彫刻の総称。 == 概要 == [[5世紀]]〜[[6世紀]]の[[福岡県]]・[[大分県]]・[[熊本県]]の古墳を中心にみられる。種類は人物（武装石人・裸体石人など）、動物（馬・鶏・猪など）、器財（靫・盾・刀・壷・蓋など）があり、赤や緑で彩色されているものもある。その種類が[[形象埴輪]]と種類が共通することから、埴輪を石にうつしたものと考えられている。 主として阿蘇溶結凝灰岩が使われるが、[[鳥取県]][[石馬谷古墳]]のものは[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使われていた。 石人石馬が種類・数ともに最も多いのは[[八女古墳群]]の[[岩戸山古墳]]で、人・動物・器財あわせて100点以上が掘り出されている == 石人石馬が出土した古墳 == * [[石馬谷古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） - 石馬（[[重要文化財]]） * [[岩戸山古墳]]（[[福岡県]][[八女市]]） - 石人・石馬・石鶏・石猪・石盾・石壺・石刀・石靫・石蓋（重要文化財） * [[乗場古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人 * [[鶴見山古墳]]（福岡県八女市） - 石人（重要文化財） * [[石人山古墳]]（福岡県[[八女郡]][[広川町 (福岡県)|広川町]]） - 石人（重要文化財） * [[石神山古墳]]（福岡県[[みやま市]]） - 石人（重要文化財） * [[臼塚古墳]]（[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]） - 石甲（重要文化財） * [[下山古墳 (臼杵市)|下山古墳]]（大分県臼杵市） - 石甲 * [[チブサン古墳]]（[[熊本県]][[山鹿市]]） - 石人 * [[三の宮古墳]]（熊本県[[荒尾市]]） - 石人 * [[江田船山古墳]]（熊本県[[玉名郡]][[和水町]]） - 石人 ※「重要文化財」は文化財保護法に基づいて指定された物件（「国の重要文化財」） == ギャラリー == ファイル:石人山古墳石人.JPG|武装石人（石人山古墳）

[[File:岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|280px|thumb|Stone Horse (excavated from Iwatozan Kofun)
Owned by Shofukuji (preserved at the Iwatozan Historical and Cultural Exchange Center)
Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture]] '''Sekijin Sekiba''' (石人石馬) refers to a collective term for stone sculptures such as human figures, horses, and other representations, erected on the mounds of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) in northern {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. == Overview == These sculptures date to the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} and are primarily found in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, and {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. The types of stone sculptures include human figures (such as armed or nude figures), animals (such as horses, chickens, and boars), and objects (such as quivers, shields, swords, pots, and lids). Some of these were painted in red or green. The variety of these items aligns with {{ill|hollow clay figures|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, leading to the interpretation that these stone sculptures were modeled after haniwa. Primarily made from {{ill|Aso welded tuff|ja|阿蘇溶結凝灰岩}}, exceptions include those made from {{ill|andesite|lt=hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}}, such as the stone horse from {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. The {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} has the largest variety and number of Sekijin Sekiba, with over 100 sculptures of people, animals, and objects unearthed. == Kofun with Excavated Sekijin Sekiba == * {{ill|Sekibadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} ({{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Yonago|ja|米子市}}) - Stone horse (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Yame|ja|八女市}}) - Stone figures, horses, chickens, boars, shields, pots, swords, quivers, and lids (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures * {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Yame City) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekibitozan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hirokawa Town|ja|広川町}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyama|ja|みやま市}}) - Stone figures (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usuki|ja|臼杵市}}) - Stone armor (Important Cultural Property) * {{ill|Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|ja|下山古墳 (臼杵市)}} (Ōita Prefecture, Usuki City) - Stone armor * {{ill|Chibusan Kofun|ja|チブサン古墳}} ({{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Yamaga|ja|山鹿市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Sannomiya Kofun|ja|三の宮古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Arao|ja|荒尾市}}) - Stone figures * {{ill|Eda Funayama Kofun|ja|江田船山古墳}} (Kumamoto Prefecture, {{ill|Nagomi Town|ja|和水町}}) - Stone figures '''Note''': Items marked as "Important Cultural Property" are designated under the {{ill|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|文化財保護法}}. == Gallery == File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|Armed Stone Figure (Sekibitozan Kofun)

[[ja:石人石馬]] [[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[en:Stone human and horse figures]][[en:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]][[sn:Stone human and horse figures]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{wikidata link|Q17994947}} ==English article== {{Short description|Type of Japanese archaeological artefact}} {{Italic title}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2025}} [[File:岩戸山古墳出土 石人 (J-831).JPG|thumb|right|6th-century {{tlit|ja|sekijin}} ([[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ICP]]) from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] ([[Tokyo National Museum]]){{cite web |url=https://emuseum.nich.go.jp/detail?content_base_id=100566 |script-title=ja:石人 |trans-title=Stone figure |language=ja, en |publisher=[[National Institutes for Cultural Heritage]] |access-date=6 March 2025}}]] {{nihongo||石人石馬|'''Sekijin sekiba'''|lead=yes}} is the inclusive term for [[sculpture]]s in stone from the [[Kofun|ancient burial mounds]] of [[Kofun period|Kofun-period]] Japan. It includes not only the stone figures of humans and horses denoted by the [[kanji]], but also depictions of other animals and objects. For this reason, these sculptures are sometimes referred to alternatively as {{nihongo||石製表飾|sekisei hyōshoku}}.{{cite book |chapter-url=https://miyazaki-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/2228 |script-title=ja:石人・石馬: 里帰り展 |trans-title=''Sekijin Sekiba'': Homecoming Exhibition |script-chapter=ja:筑紫の石製表飾(石人石馬)と古墳文化 |trans-chapter=Stone Embellishments (''Sekijin Sekiba'') of Tsukushi and Kofun Culture |author-last=Yanagisawa |author-first=Kazuo |publisher=Iwatoyama History Museum |year=1994 |pages=63–71}} ==Overview== {{tlit|ja|Sekijin sekiba}} are found mainly in northern and central [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] ([[Chikugo Province|Chikugo]], [[Higo Province|Higo]], and [[Bungo Province|Bungo]]{{cite web |url=https://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/soshiki/17/1/2/iwatoyamakouhouyame/1504594652623.html |script-title=ja:岩戸山古墳の石製表飾“石人・石馬” |trans-title=''Sekijin sekiba'' from Iwatoyama Kofun |language=ja |publisher=[[Yame|Yame City]] |access-date=7 March 2025}}) and date from the mid- to late-[[Kofun period]] ([[Century|C]]5–C6).{{rp|63}}{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031532/index.html |script-title=ja:西原古墳出土石製表飾遺物 |trans-title=Stone Embellishments Excavated from Nishihara Kofun |language=ja |publisher=[[Saga Prefecture]] |access-date=6 March 2025 |date=25 April 2023 }} Ranging in height from around {{convert|2|m}} to {{convert|30|cm}},{{rp|63}} they are understood to have been erected in front of the [[Sekkan|stone coffin]] or on top of {{tlit|ja|[[kofun]]}} together with or in place of clay {{tlit|ja|[[haniwa]]}}. These may have influenced their development—as did, in all likelihood, the statues lining the [[spirit path]]s of tombs in China.{{cite book |title=Early Japanese Art: The Great Tombs and Treasures |author-last=Kidder |first=J. Edward Jr. |author-link=J. Edward Kidder Jr. |publisher=[[Thames & Hudson]] |year=1964 |pages=82,92–94,194–195}} More than a hundred are known from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Yame]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], and sixteen from [[Nozu Kofun Cluster#Hime-no-jō Kofun|Hime-no-jō Kofun]] in [[Kumamoto Prefecture]];{{cite web |url=https://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/iwatoyama/iwaiwoshiru/11763.html |script-title=ja:4.岩戸山古墳が世界最多！「石人・石馬（石製表飾）」|trans-title=4. Iwatoyama Kofun, the most in the world! "''Sekijin sekiba (sekisei hyōshoku)''" |language=ja |publisher=[[Yame|Yame City]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} elsewhere, they are found singly or in low numbers.{{cite web |url=https://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/soshiki/4/6/3/1454651828276.html |script-title=ja:石製表飾品 |trans-title=Stone Embellishments |language=ja |publisher=[[Yame|Yame City]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} Most are carved from [[ignimbrite]] from [[Mount Aso]], though an example from [[Yonago, Tottori|Yonago]] in western Honshū is of [[andesite]] from [[Mount Daisen]].{{cite web |url=https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm |script-title=ja:国指定重要文化財 石馬 |trans-title=Nationally-designated Important Cultural Property: ''Sekiba'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Yonago, Tottori|Yonago City]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} Some retain traces of red and green pigments. Over forty examples had been catalogued by the early 1900s and in 1929 archaeologist {{ill|Morimoto Rokuji|ja|森本六爾}} published his [[monograph]] ''Sekijin Sekiba''.{{cite web |url=https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1130000796100317568 |script-title=ja:石人石馬 |trans-title=''Sekijin Sekiba'' |language=ja |website=[[CiNii]] |access-date=7 March 2025}} Suggestions as to their function include [[propitiation]] of the [[Spirit (animating force)|spirits]] of the dead, comforting the same through the accompaniment of familiar figures and things, protection of the tomb and the symbolic punishment of [[Grave robbery|robbers]], and, in the case of Iwatoyama Kofun with its "sixty stone men and sixty stone shields" {{lang|ja|「石人石盾各々六〇枚」}} per [[Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] {{tlit|ja|[[Fudoki]]}},{{rp|65}} the expression of [[Power (social and political)|power]]. Perhaps for this reason, after the [[Iwai Rebellion]] they appear to have declined somewhat in popularity, perhaps supplanted in part by the paintings of the so-called [[decorated kofun|decorated {{tlit|ja|kofun}}]]. ==Types== {{tlit|ja|Sekijin sekiba}} include sculptures of men, many of them armed, and, less commonly, women, in one instance with a child on her back;{{rp|66}} animals, including horses, boar, chickens, and waterfowl; {{tlit|ja|[[tankō]]}} and other [[Japanese armour|armour]], shields, swords, quivers, headpieces, vases, lids, a house, a chair, and a boat. ==Distribution== {{tlit|ja|Sekijin sekiba}} are known from over twenty-five sites, all but one of them in Kyūshū, the exception being {{ill|Ishiumadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in [[Yonago]], [[Tottori Prefecture]].{{rp|70}} Within Kyūshū, all but four are in the [[Prefectures of Japan|prefectures]] of [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] and [[Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto]], two being in [[Ōita Prefecture|Ōita]] ([[Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)|Shimoyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Usuzuka Kofun|ja|臼塚古墳}} in [[Usuki, Ōita|Usuki]]), with [[Saga Prefecture|Saga]] ({{ill|Nishihara Kofun|qid=Q133105161}} in [[Saga (city)|Saga]]) and [[Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki]] ([[Nobeoka]]) having one apiece.{{rp|70}} Of the ten sites in Fukuoka Prefecture, there is a concentration in [[Yame]] ([[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Noriba Kofun|Noriba Kofun]], {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}}, Dōnanzan No. 3 Kofun, Dōnanzan No. 22 Kofun, Toyofuku), the others being in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa]] ([[Yame Kofun Cluster#Sekijinsan Kofun|Sekijinsan Kofun]]), [[Kurume]] ([[Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|Ontsuka Kofun]]), [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]] ([[Sekijinsan Kofun]]), and [[Ōmuta, Fukuoka|Ōmuta]].{{rp|70}} The eleven sites in Kumamoto are in [[Arao, Kumamoto|Arao]] ({{nihongo|[[Sannomiya Kofun]]|三の宮古墳}}), [[Hikawa, Kumamoto|Hikawa]] ([[Nozu Kofun Cluster#Amazutsu Kofun|Amazutsu Kofun]] and [[Nozu Kofun Cluster#Hime-no-jō Kofun|Hime-no-jō Kofun]]), [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]] ({{nihongo|[[Futatsukasan Kofun]]|フタツカサン古墳}} and Onozaki), [[Kumamoto]] ({{nihongo|[[Ishinomuro Kofun]]|石之室古墳}}), {{nihongo|[[Tominoo Kofun]]|富ノ尾古墳}}, and Kitahara No. 1 Kofun), [[Nagomi, Kumamoto|Nagomi]] ([[Eta Funayama Kofun]]), and [[Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga]] ([[Chibusan-Obusan Kofun|Chibusan Kofun]] and [[Usuzuka Kofun (Yamaga)|Usuzuka Kofun]]).{{rp|70}} ==Important Cultural Properties== Several {{tlit|ja|sekijin sekiba}} have been [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]: * ''Sekijin'' (stone man) from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] ([[Tokyo National Museum]]){{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9962 |script-title=ja:石人 |trans-title=''Sekijin'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} * Armed ''sekijin'' from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Sekijinsan Kofun|Sekijinsan Kofun]] ([[Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa]]), Fukuoka Prefecture{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9963 |script-title=ja:武装石人 |trans-title=Armed ''Sekijin'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} * Armed ''sekijin'' from [[Sekijinsan Kofun]] ([[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]]), Fukuoka Prefecture{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9964 |script-title=ja:武装石人 |trans-title=Armed ''Sekijin'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} * Armed ''sekijin'' from {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}}, Fukuoka Prefecture ([[Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Community Center]]){{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011450 |script-title=ja:武装石人／福岡県鶴見山古墳出土 |trans-title=Armed ''Sekijin'' Excavated from Tsurumiyama Kofun, Fukuoka Prefecture |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} * 62 pieces from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] (Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Community Center){{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011206 |script-title=ja:石人／石靱／石盾　附　石人等残欠 |trans-title=''Sekijin'', Stone Quiver, and Stone Shields, together with fragments of ''Sekijin'', etc. |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}}{{cite web |url=https://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/iwatoyama/collections/tennjihin_syuuzouhin/1485415259288.html |script-title=ja:石人・石馬類 |trans-title=''Sekijin・Sekiba'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Yame|Yame City]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} * ''Sekiba'' (stone horse) from {{ill|Ishiumadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}} in [[Yonago]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] (Amenokamigaki Jinja){{rp|70}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9815 |script-title=ja:石馬 |trans-title=''Sekiba'' |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=6 March 2025}} ==Gallery== 鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|''Sekijin'' (stone man) from {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] 岩戸山古墳 武装石人 2.jpg|''Sekijin'' from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], Fukuoka Prefecture 岩戸山古墳 石馬.jpg|''Sekiba'' (stone horse) from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], Fukuoka Prefecture 石馬谷古墳出土 石馬 (複製).JPG|''Sekiba'' (replica) from {{ill|Ishiumadani Kofun|ja|石馬谷古墳}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]] ([[Kamiyodo Hakuhō-no-Oka Exhibition Hall]]) 岩戸山古墳 石盾.jpg|Shield from [[Yame Kofun Cluster#Iwatoyama Kofun|Iwatoyama Kofun]], Fukuoka Prefecture ==See also==
